summary: The most common type of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma in the orbit is a B-cell subtype, specifically MALT, follicular, or diffuse large B-cell lymphoma due to their prevalence among orbital cases. This dominance aligns with rituximab therapy efficacy against CD20-expressing B cells, confirming the B-lineage origin as central to orbital involvement.
